commands.review_summary:
The PR updates WebChat chat-controller reconciliation so visible streamed assistant text survives stale history reloads and terminal error cleanup, with controller and mocked browser E2E coverage.

PR surface: Source +56, Tests +244. Total +300 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source clears visible chatStream on stale history reload and does not materialize partial stream text on error; the PR body also cites a prior Crabbox browser repro, though I did not rerun it in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This protected maintainer PR has no narrow automated repair finding; the next action is maintainer review, CI, and optional live proof.

target.title: fix(ui): preserve visible chat stream text
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (`osolmaz`). Work is in progress until Codex review and CI are green.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Summary

WebChat could show assistant text, then make it disappear.
This change keeps visible streamed text on screen when stale history or a terminal error arrives.
The remaining confirmed part of #67035 is a WebChat state-retention bug, not a local-model latency bug.

## What Changed

The chat controller now treats visible streamed assistant text as something that must be preserved until real history replaces it.
That gives final, aborted, error, and history-reload paths the same retention rule.

- Added shared stream-retention helpers in `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts`.
- Preserved visible `chatStream` when a stale history reload does not contain replacement assistant text.
- Materialized visible streamed text into `chatMessages` before terminal error cleanup.
- Deduped preserved stream text against later final/history assistant messages.
- Strengthened mocked browser coverage for stale history during streaming and terminal error after partial output.

## Linked context

Closes #67035.

Related PR: #77418.

This follows the narrowed maintainer repro from `run_e7fc89b73ec0`, where composer preservation passed but stale refresh and terminal-error stream retention failed.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: visible WebChat assistant stream text disappearing after stale history refreshes or terminal chat errors.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS with mocked Control UI browser E2E using Google Chrome.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts --reporter=dot`
  - `PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `pnpm lint:core -- ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: controller tests pass 96/96, adjacent gateway/chat-load tests pass 72/72, mocked browser E2E passes 12/12, format/check/lint pass.
- Observed result after fix: streamed text remains visible after stale history resolves and after a terminal chat error.
- What was not tested: true Windows IME input, live LM Studio/local-model streaming, and full production browser behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this PR validates the narrowed confirmed WebChat state bug with mocked Gateway/browser proof. It does not claim to solve every symptom from the original broad issue text.
- Before evidence: Crabbox AWS browser repro `provider=aws`, lease `cbx_126c45468a1a`, run `run_e7fc89b73ec0` failed 2 of 3 checks before this patch.

## Tests and validation

I tested the controller rule directly and the browser path that previously dropped visible stream text.
The new regression coverage covers both stale history and terminal error.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm lint:core -- ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`

Regression coverage added:

- Error events now preserve partial streamed assistant text before clearing stream state.
- Error events do not duplicate streamed text when the server already supplied the same assistant message.
- Stale history reloads keep active visible streamed text.
- Stale history reloads materialize orphaned streamed text when no active run remains.
- Caught-up history clears streamed text when it contains the replacement assistant message.
- Mocked browser E2E verifies visible stream text survives stale startup history and terminal error.

## Risks

The main risk is duplicate assistant messages.
The fix mitigates that by comparing visible stream text against assistant messages after the latest user message and by deduping exact display signatures.

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: No.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: WebChat message reconciliation around stream/history ordering.
- Risk mitigation: focused controller tests plus mocked browser E2E for the two reproduced failure modes.

## Current review state

Next action: run `codex review --base origin/main`, address any P0/P1 findings, then wait for CI.

Still waiting on: Codex review and GitHub CI.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: none yet.
